real_single.text: The Court in Miranda v. Arizona, 384 U.S. 436 (1966), established a rule.

real_with_quote.text: As stated in Roe v. Wade, 410 U.S. 113 (1973), "the right of privacy is broad."

two_citations.text: See Smith v. Jones, 123 F.3d 456 (9th Cir. 2010); Doe v. Roe, 789 F.2d 12 (2d Cir. 1985).

fabricated.text: As held in Fake v. Imaginary, 999 U.S. 999 (2099), the rule applies.

no_citation.text: This answer cites no cases at all.

two_citations_with_quotes.text: The Court held "privacy is broad," Roe v. Wade, 410 U.S. 113. Elsewhere, "commerce is plenary," Gibbons v. Ogden, 22 U.S. 1.
